titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sandra Y. Govan Collection
Govan (Sandra Y.) collection of Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent interviews
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 42 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday March 21, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: Sandra Y. Govan collection of Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent interviews

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 2-4 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Sandra Y. Govan on the Bradford F. and Lila L. Swan Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: These materials are arranged into two series: I. Professional papers, 1977-1991. II. Audiovisual material, 1979-1991.

bioghist.head: Dr. Sandra Govan
bioghist.p: Dr. Sandra Y. Govan is a writer and professor emerita at the University of North Carolina in Charlotte, with a specialty in American and African-American literatures. Her areas of expertise include the Harlem Renaissance.

bioghist.head: Gwendolyn Bennett
bioghist.p: Gwendolyn Bennett (1902-1981) was an American artist, writer, and journalist who spotlighted cultural advancements during the Harlem Renaissance in the 1920s and 1930s.

bioghist.head: Richard Bruce Nugent
bioghist.p: Richard Bruce Nugent (1906-1987) was a gay African-American writer and painter lauded for his work during the Harlem Renaissance in the 1920s and 1930s.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sandra Y. Govan Collection of Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent interviews. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Sandra Y. Govan collection of Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent interviews includes correspondence, transcripts, and audiocassettes relating to Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sandra Y. Govan collection of Gwendolyn Bennett and Richard Bruce Nugent interviews is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains interview transcriptions, correspondence, and other photocopied materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 2-4 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintains order found at time of acquisition, with materials loosely grouped by interviewee, then roughly ordered chronologically within those groupings. When possible, titles are transcribed directly from the materials.

did.unittitle: 1st session - biograph stuff...outline..stops with philosophy, original
did.materialspec: Audio is unbalanced. The program is distorted at times. An echo, at times, can be heard throughout the program. High background noise is present. Static is present.
